Nelsons-Notes: - Zowe does not honor local .ssh/config. So you'll need to repeat them here '-port, -i keyfile ...'
- On `connection refused` error, check that the RSEAPI or zOSMF Service(STC/Daemon) is active and on the right port and firewall open on host vm.
- For `Rest API failures with HTTP(S) status 404` review all properties and proper JSON syntax.
- For 'Received HTTP(S) error 403 = Forbidden.'  Check if JMON is active.
- VS Code caches properties that can create issues like updating credentials or switching hosts. refresh the cache with 'cntl/P  >'  reload window.
- On TAZ/ODE, RCE uses the zOS dns name like std1.ibm.com so I added that to my local /etc/hosts as an rse host - may not be an issue after all???.
- If the cics profile errors out, run (f1) `zowe explorer: update profile ... or update the zowe.schema add cics
TODO: This is an RSE based model. It can be replace with 'type: zosmf' which requires an additional 'type: ssh' profile.  Notes pending ...

rse.COMMENT0: For zOSMF, change the above type and profile name to zosmf. And add an ssh profile and conf local keys.

cics.COMMENT1: CICS explorer uses the CICS TCPIPSERVICE STD$WUTC(CMCI) port - get it from the CICS STC sysout.

properties.COMMENT2: DPS (Profile service) port connect to the EQAPROF STC
properties.COMMENT3: RDS (Remote Client service) port connect to the EQARMTD STC. SECURED. See eqahcc.env for CCS port accessed by RDS
properties.COMMENT4: HCC Notes: Im using the EQAHCC STC which has 2 ports. One to collect (8080) one to view (8081)
properties.COMMENT4b: IVP the STC from a browser with http://zos:8081/ccaas/ping   use netstat on z to verifu the ports
properties.COMMENT4c: Use this as the VSC/CC/UI Coverage Results URL http://zos:8081/ccaas
